\begin{table}[htbp]
  \centering
  \caption{Resource footprint on the deep/nested fetch (MySQL, medians over 25
    replicates): application-process-tree CPU and database-server CPU (\% of one
    logical core), end-to-end throughput per combined (application$+$database)
    CPU-second, and peak resident memory. No layer exceeds about $110\%$ of one
    application core: the native driver runs near $100\%$ application CPU but issues
    lean SQL that drives the database server harder, while the ORMs sit near
    $100\%$ application CPU at lower database cost, so there is no
    CPU-for-throughput trade. Per-layer efficiency and the equal-CPU control are in
    Supplement Tables~S5 and~S4.}
  \label{tab:resources_main}
  \begin{tabular}{l r r r r}
    \toprule
    Layer & app CPU\% & db CPU\% & req/comb-CPU-s & RSS (MB) \\
    \midrule
    mysql2 & 105 & 85 & 1254 & 294 \\
    knex & 100 & 75 & 1147 & 215 \\
    drizzle & 105 & 50 & 850 & 297 \\
    prisma & 105 & 60 & 384 & 334 \\
    sequelize & 100 & 30 & 682 & 262 \\
    typeorm & 100 & 70 & 598 & 225 \\
    objection & 100 & 45 & 575 & 221 \\
    mikroorm & 110 & 25 & 356 & 356 \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}
